top_stories.title: SailPoint to Acquire Entro in Reported $200 Million Deal
top_stories.summary: SailPoint is acquiring Israel-based Entro Security in a reported $200 million deal. Entro specializes in non-human identity (NHI) and secrets security, and the acquisition will expand SailPoint's identity governance capabilities into the NHI space.

top_stories.title: What is new in Microsoft Entra: June 2026 updates
top_stories.summary: Microsoft Entra's June 2026 update introduces phishing-resistant MFA for Linux desktops, expanded passkey support on Windows, and changes to Self-Service Password Reset identity verification. The release also includes new Conditional Access enforcement changes affecting authentication policy management across the platform.

top_stories.title: IBM and Microsoft partner to automate identity threat response
top_stories.summary: IBM and Microsoft have launched a collaborative service that integrates Microsoft's security telemetry with IBM's managed response services to automate identity threat detection and remediation. The partnership is designed to bridge the gap between identifying identity-based threats and executing governed remediation actions.

top_stories.title: Microsoft enhances Entra and Defender to counter AI-driven identity attacks
top_stories.summary: Microsoft has enhanced its Entra and Defender products to counter AI-driven identity attacks. The updates address AI-automated reconnaissance, personalized social engineering, and chained vulnerability exploitation targeting identity systems.

top_stories.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Exposes Software Development Pipeline Risk
top_stories.summary: A leaked GitHub token at Novo Nordisk exposed vulnerabilities in the company's software development pipeline, highlighting secrets management failures. The incident illustrates how organizations misclassify secrets management as a tooling problem rather than an identity and access management problem.

news.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
news.summary: Klue, a market intelligence platform, confirmed a security breach in which threat actors stole OAuth tokens used to connect to customers' Salesforce environments. The attack was claimed by a new extortion group called "Icarus," and the victim list is reportedly growing.

news.title: Every AI Agent Is an Identity. Most Organizations Don't Treat Them That Way
news.summary: Token Security published an analysis arguing that AI agents represent a new class of identity requiring governance, as they can access data, trigger workflows, deploy code, and interact with critical systems with little oversight. The piece frames AI agents as an emerging identity and access management challenge, highlighting gaps in how organizations currently handle non-human identities.

news.title: Webinar: How attackers bypass MFA and how defenders can respond
news.summary: A webinar addresses how modern phishing techniques, including Device Code phishing, can bypass MFA protections to gain unauthorized access to corporate accounts. The session covers behavioral AI approaches for detecting compromised accounts and automating response workflows.

news.title: Klue breach lead to Salesforce data theft, Huntress affected
news.summary: A breach at Klue, a market intelligence platform, began with a single compromised integration credential and cascaded into theft of customer data across multiple connected platforms including Salesforce, affecting cybersecurity vendor Huntress among others. The incident illustrates supply chain credential compromise and the risks of interconnected SaaS integrations, with Huntress publishing a detailed post-incident account on June 18.

news.title: Forget traffic lights, Google’s reCAPTCHA may ask for hand gestures
news.summary: Google has introduced hand gesture verification as a new challenge method for its reCAPTCHA service, used to distinguish humans from bots. The feature is deployed on login pages, registration forms, password reset pages, and checkout systems as part of Google Cloud Fraud Defense.

news.title: Forget Data Leakage: Shadow AI's Real Threat Is Access Control
news.summary: The article argues that shadow AI has evolved from a data leakage problem into an access control problem for enterprises. The focus is on how unsanctioned AI tools are creating unauthorized access pathways, framing the issue as an identity and access management challenge rather than a data loss prevention one.

news.title: Salesforce Disables Klue App Integration After OAuth Token Abuse Exposes Customer Data
news.summary: Salesforce disabled the Klue Battlecards app integration after a security incident on June 11, 2026, involving OAuth token abuse that exposed customer data. The incident resulted in organizations being unable to connect to Salesforce via the Klue app until further notice, highlighting risks associated with OAuth-based third-party integrations.

news.title: FIFA Bug Exposes World Cup Streams to Remote Takeover
news.summary: A security vulnerability in FIFA's systems exposed World Cup streams to potential remote takeover due to unenforced Microsoft Entra access controls. The flaw could have allowed an attacker to hijack broadcast streams by exploiting misconfigured identity and access management controls in FIFA's Entra deployment.

news.title: Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks
news.summary: Klue, a market intelligence platform, suffered an OAuth breach that allowed threat actors known as "Icarus" to steal Salesforce CRM data from multiple organizations. The attack is part of an ongoing extortion campaign exploiting compromised OAuth credentials to gain unauthorized access to connected systems.

news.title: Orphaned AI Agents: How to Find Hidden Access Risks Inside Your Network
news.summary: The article addresses the emerging IAM challenge of orphaned AI agents that retain standing privileges after their creators leave an organization. It focuses on how enterprises lack visibility and governance over AI agent access to sensitive resources, framing it as an identity governance and access management problem.

news.title: Zero-Touch OAuth for MCP
news.summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has released a stable Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension that enables centralized provisioning of MCP server access through an organization's identity provider. The extension eliminates per-app OAuth flows by automatically connecting users to authorized MCP servers upon first login, integrating AI tool access management with existing IdP infrastructure.

news.title: Google Toldl Researchers 'Nice Catch!' Then Denied Bug Bounty For Flaw It Still Hasn't Fixed
news.summary: Security researcher Justin O'Leary discovered a privilege escalation vulnerability in Google's Config Connector that Google initially classified as high-priority and high-severity. Google subsequently denied the bug bounty by declaring the behavior "working as intended" while leaving the flaw unpatched.

news.title: Microsoft Entra ID single sign-on for the Starburst connector in Power BI (Preview)
news.summary: Microsoft has released a preview feature enabling Entra ID single sign-on for the Starburst connector in Power BI. When enabled, report viewers querying semantic models in DirectQuery mode authenticate to Starburst using their own Entra ID identity, with access governed by Starburst's row-level and column-level security policies.

news.title: Semperis Appoints Jimmy McNary as Deputy Federal CTO to Drive Identity Security Strategy Across the Public Sector
news.summary: Semperis has appointed Jimmy McNary as Deputy Federal CTO to lead identity security strategy for its public sector division. McNary is a cybersecurity veteran with 25+ years of experience, previously serving as VP of Federal Solutions.

news.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
news.summary: A large-scale credential-harvesting campaign has compromised more than 30,000 Fortinet devices across nearly 200 countries. Attackers have compiled a list of working credentials from the breached devices, targeting organizations across multiple sectors.

news.title: Microsoft Confirms RoguePlanet Defender Zero-Day, Says Patch is in Development
news.summary: Microsoft has confirmed a zero-day vulnerability in Microsoft Defender, designated CVE-2026-50656 with a CVSS score of 7.8, which involves a privilege escalation flaw in the Microsoft Malware Protection Engine. A patch is currently in development, with the vulnerability internally codenamed RoguePlanet.

news.title: The Top 10 Attack Surface Exposures in 2026
news.summary: An article outlines the top 10 attack surface exposures projected for 2026, including exposed admin panels, credential reuse, and vulnerabilities that expose credentials and session tokens from server memory. The piece covers broadly relevant security topics including authentication weaknesses and credential-based attack vectors.

news.title: IDMWORKS Transforms Identity and Access Management with Innovative Accelerator
news.summary: IDMWORKS has launched Identity Matrix, a patent-pending IAM delivery accelerator that consolidates four enterprise identity projects into a single solution. The tool includes built-in security checks, privileged-account discovery, and machine-identity classification capabilities.

news.title: Show HN: AuthPlane – OAuth 2.1 and PKCE authorization server for MCP
news.summary: AuthPlane is an open-source OAuth 2.1 and PKCE authorization server designed for the Model Context Protocol (MCP). The project was shared on Hacker News with minimal engagement (4 points, no comments).

news.title: Why Account Takeovers Are Rising and How to Stop Them
news.summary: Specops Software published an analysis on the rise of account takeover attacks, examining attack vectors including phishing, session hijacking, and MFA fatigue. The article explores device trust and continuous verification as countermeasures to reduce account takeover risk.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords, successfully defacing high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force's Chief Master Sergeant. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to bypass account authentication were circulated on Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a significant credential leak. Congressional members from both chambers are demanding answers while CISA works to contain the breach and invalidate the exposed credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed credentials for multiple highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and internal CISA systems in a public GitHub repository. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant privileged credential exposure incident.
